headline: Health Impact Assessments Required for Only 8% of New Developments
context: Most major development projects proceed without mandatory health impact assessment despite environmental health risks.

cards.big: Only 8% of major development projects undergo health impact assessment before approval
cards.sub: EIA process does not include mandatory health component for most project categories

cards.big: Environmental Quality Act 1974 requires EIA but health assessment is optional not mandatory
cards.sub: Health impacts are afterthoughts rather than prerequisites in development planning

cards.big: 5 industrial development projects approved in 2025 later linked to community health complaints
cards.sub: Post-hoc health problems are more expensive to address than pre-approval prevention

cards.big: Wales mandates health impact assessment for all policies and major projects since 2015
cards.sub: Upstream health protection proved cost-effective at GBP1 saved for every GBP0.14 invested

cards.big: Approving developments without assessing health impact is gambling with community wellbeing
Should health impact assessments be mandatory for all major development project approvals
